required_skills: High-performance computing (HPC) simulation development in Fortran, C, C++, or Python
Numerical methods including finite element method (FEM), finite difference method (FDM), and finite volume method (FVM)
Parallel programming using MPI, OpenMP, or CUDA for distributed and shared-memory scientific computing
Mathematical modelling of physical, biological, or chemical systems using differential equations and continuum mechanics
Algorithm design and complexity analysis for computationally intensive scientific applications
Scientific data analysis and visualisation of large-scale simulation outputs using Python or MATLAB
Verification and validation (V&V) of computational models against experimental and analytical benchmarks
Software engineering practices for scientific codes including modular design, version control, and regression testing
Multiscale and multiphysics simulation methods for coupled physical systems
Monte Carlo methods and stochastic simulation techniques for uncertainty quantification
preferred_skills: Experience with quantum chemistry or molecular dynamics simulation packages (GROMACS, LAMMPS, Gaussian)
Knowledge of machine learning force fields and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) for scientific simulation
Familiarity with GPU-accelerated simulation frameworks and performance portability tools (RAJA, Kokkos)
Experience with mesh generation and adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) strategies
Knowledge of spectral methods, discontinuous Galerkin (DG) methods, or lattice Boltzmann methods
Familiarity with cloud-based HPC environments (AWS ParallelCluster, Google Cloud HPC)
Experience with scientific workflow management tools (Snakemake, Nextflow, Pegasus)
frameworks: PETSc (Portable Extensible Toolkit for Scientific Computation)
responsibilities: Develop and maintain high-performance computational simulation codes in C++, Fortran, or Python, implementing numerical solvers for partial differential equations governing physical or biological systems
Design and optimise parallel algorithms using MPI and OpenMP to scale simulations across thousands of HPC cores, achieving efficient weak and strong scaling performance
Implement and validate numerical methods including FEM, FDM, and FVM discretisations, verifying solver accuracy against analytical solutions and experimental benchmark datasets
Conduct large-scale simulation campaigns on national HPC facilities (e.g., ARCHER2, Summit, Frontier), managing job submission, resource allocation, and post-processing workflows
Perform uncertainty quantification (UQ) and sensitivity analysis using Monte Carlo, quasi-Monte Carlo, and polynomial chaos expansion methods to characterise model prediction confidence
Develop multiphysics coupling frameworks to integrate simulation codes across fluid dynamics, structural mechanics, heat transfer, and chemistry domains for coupled system analysis
Analyse and visualise terabyte-scale simulation output data using ParaView, VisIt, and custom Python scripts, generating publication-quality figures and animations for scientific communication
Establish model verification and validation (V&V) frameworks, defining quantitative accuracy metrics and systematically comparing simulation predictions against experimental reference data
Collaborate with domain scientists and experimental researchers to translate physical problem statements into tractable computational models with well-defined boundary conditions and assumptions
Profile and optimise computational codes using Intel VTune or Valgrind to identify memory bottlenecks, cache inefficiencies, and parallelism limitations in scientific simulation kernels
